title: neolitsea impressa
intro: Neolitsea impressa, also known as the Indian laurel or West Indian bay tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. Traditionally, the leaves and bark of Neolitsea impressa have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Neolitsea impressa include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions, among others. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or used as a tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
